commit.message:
Fix game hang by correcting CPU branch timing

Branch taken (+1) and page-crossing (+2) cycle penalties were lost because
branch() incremented this.cycles but execute() overwrote it with the base
cost. Use a branchPenalty field that gets added after execute() returns.
Also remove temporary debug instrumentation from PPU and NesEmulator.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add component diagram in draw.io format

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix game hang after screensaver by delivering APU frame counter IRQs to CPU

The APU's isIrqPending() was never checked in the main loop, so games
relying on APU IRQs for timing (e.g. Double Dragon & Battletoads) would
spin indefinitely waiting for an interrupt that never fired.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add APU implementation for sound support

Implement NES Audio Processing Unit with pulse, triangle, and noise
channels, wired into the CPU bus and main emulation loop. Audio output
uses javax.sound.sampled at 44100 Hz.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix game freeze after title screen by adding NMI edge detection, PRG RAM, and DMA NMI handling

- Trigger NMI immediately when game enables NMI via PPUCTRL while VBlank is active
- Add 8KB PRG RAM at $6000-$7FFF so game state reads/writes work correctly
- Check for NMI during DMA transfer to avoid missing VBlank interrupts

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add README with architecture overview, setup, and API docs

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Implement notification system with server cluster, workers, emulators, and load testing

- Notification server: REST API, input validation, template rendering, Redis caching, RabbitMQ routing
- Notification worker: 4 dedicated queue consumers (APNS/FCM/SMS/EMAIL) with retry logic
- Third-party emulator: simulates delivery services with 95% success rate
- Device emulator: receives notifications, WebSocket broadcast, REST stats
- Load client: seeds test data, multi-threaded load test with p95/p99 reporting
- Infrastructure: PostgreSQL, Redis, RabbitMQ, Prometheus, Grafana with pre-provisioned dashboard
- Docker Compose with service health checks and replicas
- 27 unit tests covering validation, services, controllers, consumers
- Component diagram in drawio format

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: correct api test mocks for jsdom environment and flexible header matching

Use jsdom's native window instead of stubbing globalThis, and match headers
with objectContaining to avoid brittleness from additional default headers.

docs(phase-5): complete phase execution — all 9 SWIP reqs verified, 18/18 must-haves

commit.message:
fix: Move share_token index from schema to migration for existing DBs

The CREATE UNIQUE INDEX on playbook_plays(share_token) in schema.sql
fails on Railway because the table already exists without the column.
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS skips creation, then the index references
a non-existent column. The migration code already creates this index
after adding the column via ALTER TABLE.

[KAN-DB] Fix timezone: 'today' filter uses 24h rolling window instead of UTC midnight

The "today" date filter was using UTC midnight as the cutoff, which meant
when it's still March 7 in the US but already March 8 UTC, jobs created
on March 7 would be excluded. Changed to a 24-hour rolling window so
"today" always includes recent jobs regardless of timezone differences.

Fix: only resolve orgId for admin users, tighten recipientId DTO

resolveOrgId was called unconditionally in listConversations and
findConversation, causing 400 errors for users without orgId in their
JWT. Non-admin queries use userId only — orgId is now resolved only
when the admin code path needs it.

Also adds Transform to strip empty-string recipientId before validation.

une transaction unique.

extraction.

recipient visibility probes.

mapping.

commit.message:
feat: fork as ableton-flow with critical bug fixes

Fixes from original ahujasid/ableton-mcp:

- server.py: remove fake sendall(b'') health check — TCP sends of empty
  bytes never test if the connection is actually alive
- server.py: always call close() before setting sock=None to avoid
  leaving zombie connections that freeze Ableton's Remote Script
- server.py: add _run() helper with auto-reconnect on any command failure
- server.py: per-command timeout (20s modifying, 10s read-only)
- Remote Script: replace settimeout(None) with 60s timeout so recv()
  doesn't block forever on unexpected client disconnect
- Remote Script: explicit handling of ConnectionResetError/BrokenPipeError
- Remote Script: always break after unexpected errors (no silent loop)
- Remote Script: call shutdown(SHUT_RDWR) + close() in finally block
- pyproject.toml: rename package to ableton-flow

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Classify LabelAndBlockNameErrors → §9.3.5 [skip ci]

LRM §9.3.5 "Statement labels" explicitly states: "It shall be illegal to have both a label before a begin or fork and a block name after the begin or fork." The test exercises exactly this rule — `my_label: begin : block_name` uses both a statement label and a block name on the same begin block.

commit.message:
feat: SQLite journal support, git context fixes, atomic ops hardening, quiet mode, and robustness improvements

SQLite rollback journal (.db-journal) support:
- Document .db-journal files in INSTALLING.md and SYNC_MAINTENANCE_CHECKLIST.md
- Handle journal files in init, config, sync, and storage layers
- Add test coverage for journal file scenarios

Git command context awareness:
- changelog, orphans, and stats commands now resolve the git repo root
  from the target project path instead of relying on CWD, fixing incorrect
  results when invoked from outside the tracked repository

--db override refactor:
- External DB paths now fall back to workspace discovery instead of
  returning a hard error, improving UX when the override path is stale
  or inaccessible

Config command error propagation:
- config get and config delete now propagate DB open failures upstream
  instead of silently swallowing them, surfacing actionable errors

--quiet mode support:
- Added --quiet flag to update, upgrade, and version commands
- Suppresses non-essential output for scripted/CI usage
- E2E tests added for quiet mode behavior

Atomic file operations hardening:
- Introduced TempFileGuard RAII pattern for automatic cleanup on failure
- Use create_new(true) to avoid TOCTOU races
- Added integrity checks before rename in export, snapshot save/load,
  and history backup paths

Database recovery robustness:
- Extracted generic rollback_renamed_paths and rename_existing_paths
  helpers to reduce duplication
- Proper rollback on partial staging failures prevents corrupt state

Sync path validation:
- New NonRegularFile error variant for clearer diagnostics
- Reject directories and symlinks at sync entry point with descriptive
  errors instead of cryptic I/O failures downstream

Graph command improvements:
- Emit proper error when dependency metadata is missing instead of
  panicking
- Fix cycle root detection to correctly identify the entry node

Dependency metadata query hardening:
- Handle external prefix in dep lookups correctly
- Return proper errors for missing internal targets instead of
  Option::unwrap panics

Transaction side-effect rollback:
- Issue explicit ROLLBACK when post-closure side-effect operations fail,
  preventing half-committed state in the database

Query command JSON format propagation:
- Error rendering now respects the --json flag, emitting structured
  error objects instead of plain text when JSON output is requested

Enable model invocation for low-risk skills

effects. All other skills remain user-invoked only.

commit.message:
[Instrument] Geometry analysis — meat on the bones

analyze_geometry.py computes all metrics the paper describes:
- Word count vs displacement correlation: SOUL r=-0.40 (confirmed), design_notes r=0.913 (confirmed)
- Drift vectors: SOUL -72.2deg, essays +37.2deg (~109deg apart, independent)
- Wander ratios: essays 16.33 (wide search), SOUL 3.53, design_notes 2.38 (directed)
- Inter-family centroid distances: essays<->design_notes 8.22->4.47
- Convergence is linear (R2=0.954) not exponential
- Superposition: 34/46 evolution docs between two document-type centroids (epsilon=0.05)
- Wallas signature: 91.85% contemplative->synthesis within 5 turns (319 events)
- SOUL 768-dim sequential sim: min=0.909 at v07 (hinge confirmed in full space)
- Phase transitions at steps 7, 9, 17 (all above mean+1std threshold)

geometry_analysis.json: full output including classified turns, all distance timeline

paper_skeleton updated with computed values — corrections to visual estimates:
- Inter-family distances clarified (centroid vs specific pair)
- Drift angles corrected (-72deg/-58deg SOUL, +37deg/+40deg essays)
- Convergence fit result added (linear beats exponential)
- Wallas quantitative finding inserted (91.85%)
- Hinge 768-dim confirmation inserted

commit.message:
Live dashboard v2: frozen normalization, MT5-exact sizing, enhanced data capture

- Frozen normalization mode with feature baseline (prevents conviction collapse from market shocks)
- Adaptive normalizer with outlier clamping (6σ) and auto-reset detector as fallback
- MT5-exact lot sizing via calc_profit API + pre-flight margin validation
- Enhanced trade executor: profit protection stages, re-entry penalty, spread filtering
- Rich data capture: trades.jsonl with entry features, SL trail history, reward components
- Actions.jsonl with block reasons, protection stage tracking
- Auto-generated weekly summaries (Friday 22:00 UTC)
- Feature pipeline with feature_index.json and health validation
- Broker constraints module for dynamic spread/session checks
- Week 1 demo performance report
- Logger enhancements for structured JSONL output

commit.message:
Add daily cron job to renew Clio webhooks before expiry

Clio webhooks expire after 7 days. Previously they only renewed when
a webhook event was received or the settings page was visited. This
adds a Vercel cron that runs daily at 3am to proactively renew any
webhooks expiring within 48 hours.

- DB RPCs: get_expiring_clio_webhooks, update_clio_webhook (SECURITY DEFINER)
- API route: /api/cron/renew-webhooks (secured by CRON_SECRET)
- vercel.json with cron schedule
- Middleware updated to allow /api/cron/ as public route

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: use configured gate_threshold in remote inject path

The remote inject_context_remote() and its search fallback were using a
hardcoded 0.005 gate threshold instead of the configured hooks.gate_threshold
(default 0.50). This meant all remote-mode agents (everyone using --server)
had effectively no quality gate on injected context, causing 85% noise rate
in feedback.

Now both remote paths read the gate from config/CLI args, matching the local
path behavior.

commit.message:
feat: auto-inject session memory into every agent prompt

The critical fix: memory is now injected directly into each agent's prompt
before the run starts -- not relying on the agent remembering to call a tool.

Same pattern as the vault (CLAUDE.md + MEMORY.md are always present).
Context compression can't remove what was never in the context window to begin with.

- runner.ts: loadMemoryContext() reads attack-logs/memory/*.md and prepends to prompt
- All 9 prompts: START section updated -- memory is auto-loaded, not tool-called
- memory_read() tool still available for mid-run queries if needed
